collection_method: Public first-party product pages and their storefront review feed; full text retained, HTML removed, reviewer names omitted.

reviews.title: Absolutely loved this meal
reviews.text: Absolutely loved this meal. We created our own Gyro using the Chimchurri Beef, Tzatziki sauce, sweet peppers, and onions. Absolutely delicious!

reviews.title: I’m not one usually give a review
reviews.text: I’m not one usually give a review and we love this brand as a family but when we purchased the Chimichurri Beef we fully expected the meat to be slices and not wild chunks of beef. The flavor was good but the presentation and beef outcome had extreme lack luster. How can you show slices of steak on a photo and the outcome was more like a can of dintymore. Again, we enjoy this brand about 90% of the time but this unfortunately fell into that 10% of disappointment.

reviews.title: Taste was ok, beef was ok. Contains coconut!!!
reviews.text: Taste was ok, beef was ok. There should be larger warning on the label in front about the allergens. Coconut affects a lot of people who have peanut allergies. Caused a pretty bad reaction for my son.

reviews.title: I was skeptic but pleasantly surprised!
reviews.text: Honestly, I was skeptic at first but bought it in a pinch to feed my husband and I while travelling and we were tired of eating out everyday, especially since I have Celiac and find it hard to eat out. I bought corn tortillas, sliced cheese and brocoli sprouts and made delicious beef tacos. Didn't need any salsa or hot sauce and was able to make what looked like a gourmet taco meal from my hotel microwave! Made the same thing the next day! The convenience, clean ingredients, celiac safe AND the great flavor really checked off the boxes for us.

reviews.title: The beef is hit or miss :(
reviews.text: I always have a good experience with the chicken, but the sous vide beef options are hit or miss. There is so much bristle in there i end up only getting about 2/3 of the meat that's edible! And for $13 it should be better quality. But the sauces are always good

reviews.title: Delicious and really simple to make
reviews.text: Delicious and really simple to make. I mixed it with broccoli and just like the package says 5 minute meal. Great for busy schedules and you wants a quick clean meal. LOVE IT

reviews.title: Bought the Hawaiian Chicken and loved it all
reviews.text: Bought the Hawaiian Chicken and loved it all though the weight was off by 3 ounces. Decided to try this, since the kids were home bought 2. Yuck. The quality is awful. Meat is gummy others have referred to it as dog food appearance, I agree. Again your weights are off. In this economy chosing to buy quality and being shorted on product is very bad. I bought 4, 2 went to the dog- she loved it, Hawaiian Chicken winner off with weight, honey garlic winner but again off in weight. We weigh everything due to diet.

reviews.title: Convenience isn't everything
reviews.text: The sauce isn't bad. The meat feels glued together. There is something that tastes strongly of chemicals, maybe from the packaging. I would sacrifice convenience to preserve the meat texture and quality.

reviews.title: It’s the best ready to eat frozen meal quick to prepare
reviews.text: It’s the best ready to eat frozen meal quick to cook in 5 to 10 minutes’ it’s one of our favorites . I usually buy 2 packages whenever it’s on sale that’s the only way I can afford it. It’s much better and healthier meals so when it was on sale at Publix so bought variety meals which I can keep in my freezer for at least couple of months. Than you for making our life easier especially in our kitchen.

reviews.title: Sirloin Steak Tips A+
reviews.text: Loved everything about the sirloin steak tips. I cooked the beef in a frying pan w olive oil... to just well done. While also cutting the meat into much smaller pieces. Added the gravy and cooked only until heated because I didn't want the sauce to dry up. Then served it on rice. Perfect. Tender beef lightly salted. I find it interesting that other reviews said it needed more salt... well, use your salt shaker. That's a ridiculous complaint. I will definitely make this again. I had half for dinner reserving half for next day's lunch.

reviews.title: Taste is good. Serving size disappointing.
reviews.text: Taste is good. I’m not sure I appreciate the chewy texture others do. However, packaging states 16oz, however when you weigh it, it’s only about 10oz. Can barely feed my husband & myself.

reviews.title: Nothing like the packaging presents
reviews.text: Listed as beef strips, but looks more like ground beef. Very misleading from what the package represented.

reviews.title: I can’t take hot peppers so when I saw
reviews.text: I can’t take hot peppers so when I saw this Mongolian style beef and I read the ingredients that there was no peppers in it. I was all excited but still this is really hot for me so do you guys have red peppers in this cause it’s not listed in the ingredients?

reviews.title: Second Dog Food !
reviews.text: Husband was disappointed I bought this and opened to see what others have noted. Does NOT look like the package pictured. It indeed looks like gray ground dog food. Will not buy again.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text: I pretty much live on chicken, so I decided to try this as an alternative. I was quite disappointed. I rarely salt anything for health reasons, but felt this needed it. Despite following the directions carefully, the meat was chewy and not particularly flavorful. The label said three servings, but that’s a stretch. I divided it into two portions. I wanted to love it, but for about $15, I would not buy it again.

reviews.title: Gray dog food
reviews.text: It comes out of the package looking like gray dog food. You can kind of preserve chunks but certainly not strips nor anything like the photos. The sauce is ok. You need a lot of it to make it palatable. The alleged average score is not credible for this product. Discontinue or recreate this.

reviews.title: Way better than you think it will be
reviews.text: I first tried a Kevin beef and broccoli at Costco. It was pretty good, I do choose to cook the beef longer than recommended. I then tried the beef bulgogi. Wow. Amazing. Better than any Asian food I’ve ever had making it at home that doesn’t costs hundreds of dollars. I’ll be a regular Kevin’s customer for convenient easy to make dinners when I get home from work and my lady is already asleep. This is better than you think it will be. I just wish I could try more meals at my grocery store. There’s a few chicken ones, I haven’t tried those. Probably will I’m just a beef guy.

reviews.title: Tasty? Sort of but tough
reviews.text: Bought at Whole Foods $16 not right. Meat was a bit tough. Overall, too salty. Won’t buy again.

reviews.title: Needs a lot of rescuing, doesn't deliver 16 oz of food.
reviews.text: First, they show 16 oz net weight, so I weighed the sauce and meat packages. Sauce was 5.55 oz, Meat 11.2 oz, but right away you're told to drain the broth, which in my case was over 3.6 oz, so the total was closer to 13 oz. Not even considering the slight weight of the pouches, or dehydration during cooking, that's still about 20% of a pound underweight. Second, the appearance of the beef in the pouch is like a lump of clay. They suggest using tongs to separate the beef. On the package they look like tidy little slices, but really, they're so pressed together that they don't want to come apart. I finally used to forks to tease apart what I could, and none of the resultant pieces were "slices," more like lumps chunks. Cooking did nothing to help. Third, there's too much sauce for this little amount of beef. It's about I added about two cups of stir-fried asparagus, baby corn, baby carrots and mushrooms, and it was still dripping in sauce. Fourth, that's what I mean by "lots of resucing." It needed the added flavors. Fifth, neither honey or garlic flavors can be detected in the sauce, and it's not surprising. In a list of 16 ingredients, Honey is fifth, behind two doses of water. Garlic is seventh (roasted) and ninth (dehydrated). As others have pointed out, the orange juice concentrate, number six on the list, is the strongest flavor. Sixth, the texture of the beef is horrible, tough and chewy; the flavor is like an overcooked pot roast.

reviews.title: Upset I didn't buy more!
reviews.text: I purchased this maybe a year ago or more, threw it in the freezer and didn't give it much thought. Took it out of the freezer the other night and tried it. I am OBSESSED!!! It's so so delish! I went to the store the next day looking for it, with the plan to purchase all they had, I loved it so much and to my surprise, there was none! I have searched all over my area, at different stores and no one has it anymore 🥺 please restock or send me 20 packages of the Mongolian Beef please and thank you!

reviews.title: very disappointed
reviews.text: My family loves all your chicken dishes and I thought I would try a beef dish. I tried your sirloin steak tips with gravy and was very disappointed in the quality of the meat. It was very chewy and had no flavor at all. I guess I will stick with the chicken .

reviews.title: JAW WORKOUT
reviews.text: If you want strong jaw muscles this is the recipe for you. I dont know what Xanthan gum is but with how this chewed id bet it is the main ingredient, not beef. Gravy tasted fine.

reviews.title: Kevin beef
reviews.text: Had this for supper. Very good. Bought at Costco don’t remember price but little high for what you get. Would have liked more meat.

reviews.title: So Good
reviews.text: Bought this at Costco a few days ago and decided to finally try it for a quick meal. It was so amazing. The flavor was great and added some grilled onions to it which made it even better. Definitely trying other flavors and buying this again!

reviews.title: Disappointing - Meat was very grisly and very tough to chew
reviews.text: I'll start with the good. The flavor was decent but hardly "Korean BBQ". More on the lines of Hawaiian BBQ- sweeter, not spicy at all, but still tasty. The meat, however, was hard to get through... it was so tough, I ended up giving most to my dog. I get the idea of sous vide, but it really doesn't do much for making a tough cut of meat tender. I think you'd be better off pressure cooking the meat before packaging.

reviews.title: Yummy!
reviews.text: We enjoyed this over rice and veggies. Will buy again

reviews.title: Serving size inaccurate
reviews.text: This says three 5oz servings. I was splitting for meal prep and only had 8oz TOTAL. Not 15oz. The beef was tough and lacking flavor. Flavor is one thing, but to charge for a quantity that's not delivered is deceptive/theft.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text: Meat was mushy even with searing per the instructions. Sauce was overwhelmingly sweet and not a Korean BBQ marinade at all - why put lemongrass in it (not a traditional KP bbq ingredient and threw the taste off). Had high hopes but left confused.

reviews.title: Korean BBQ Beef
reviews.text: This product is a huge disappointment! What kind of beef is this?? Grisly, veins, muscle??? I ended up throwing half the meat away. I had a bad batch the first time I bought this product yet decided to give it another try. Never again!

reviews.title: Yuuuum dog food
reviews.text: Literally dog food. But nobody will ever read this review because Kevin's filters only positive ones

reviews.title: Delicious but Xanthan Gum is a Problem
reviews.text: I was excited to try your Sirloin Steak Tips with Gravy and thought I'd found a quick meal solution. It was delicious, but xanthan gum causes gastrointestinal distress in some people including me. (BTW, I'm not sensitive to other food ingredients.) I thought it wouldn't be a problem since it's low on your ingredient list, but I was wrong. Such a shame. I don't understand why companies use xantham gum instead of better thickeners/stabilizers. Perhaps it's cheaper. Sadly (for both of us), you've lost me as a customer.

reviews.title: Horrible Steak Tips and Gravy
reviews.text: The Steak Tips are chewy and have no seasoning flavor. Absolutely a waste of money to buy. And the sauce packet needs to be increased. Not enough sauce.

reviews.title: NOT korean style at all
reviews.text: As a Korean, I find it frustrating that this product is marketed as “Korean BBQ style” when it doesn’t accurately reflect the flavors of Korean cuisine.

reviews.title: Awful
reviews.text: The steak is chewy and don't get me started on the "Korean bbq" taste. Literally tastes like normal BBQ sauce. I had to doctor it with my own Asian seasonings and I still couldn't mask the crappy flavor. Glad I got it on sale or I would be more annoyed. Talk to a real Korean about how to flavor your food and try again.

reviews.title: Yucky
reviews.text: We had high hopes for a gluten free and healthy quick meal. Thankfully we got it on sale at Costco, because that is still overpriced for this meal! Most of the pieces had to be spit out because they were tough with the wrong texture! We will never buy this again. Such a big bummer!

reviews.title: Very Dissatisfied
reviews.text: I had hopes for this meal, but none of my family liked it. Money down the drain.

reviews.title: Super tasty but a little sweet
reviews.text: Really enjoyed this as a quick meal with a side of rice and broccoli. I did find the meat a little tough and the sauce a little in the sweet side but overall a great quick meal!

reviews.title: disappointing
reviews.text: I had been looking forward to this. Definitely not steak tips as advertised, they were really small bits of beef rather than tips. Followed the directions - the pieces were chewy, had to spit out a couple of pieces. Bought it at Costco on sale. I'm so glad I didn't pay full price for this.

reviews.title: Dog food Scraps
reviews.text: This was one of the worst premade products I have ever had. It’s so bad, that this is the first review about any food item that I’ve ever written. 1/4th of the package weight is the sauce. So the 2 lb package for $18 (hawaii price) is actually more like $11 a lbs instead of $9 a lb. That would be fine if the meat wasn’t absolute gristle. Their “sous vide” method gives it look like dogfood and a texture something like beef jerky that was boiled, dried again, and steamed. It’s not even seasoned. Sous vide is for even cooking of usually larger things… not thin scraps of meat. This “sous vide” is not a culinary offering as much as it is a time and cost saving measure. Why would they cook and season it properly and then Vacuum seal it, when you just can boil the unseasoned meat and pack the sauce on the side For $11 per lb, go buy strip streak and cut it yourself. This is a company taking scraps of an already cheap cut and ripping off its customers. Absolute garbage and they should be embarrassed. I don’t even want anything from them… I just feel like people need to know how much this company doesn’t care about you.

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.text: Recently had the Hawaiian chicken and it was super good so decided to try this flavor. Bought both packages at Costco so makes meal prep all week easy since it’s bigger packages. This Korean BBQ Beef missed the mark. Great flavor! Usually that’s the issue. Not with this. The steak is dense (hard to even separate the block, had to hand tear as plastic silverware kept bending), stringy, grisly, and not at all tender. This would be really good with a different cut of meat that is more tender as the flavor is really good!

reviews.title: Yeeeeaaaa………….No
reviews.text: I was looking for something to pair with my eggs and I know Korean style beef is usually tender, so I picked this up today just for the meat to be very chewy and I came across pieces of fat. I know that could be normal with meat sometimes , but it says it was sous vide? So that plus the fat I don’t know but maybe that was just my experience

reviews.title: Beef Scraps
reviews.text: My meat looked like a clump of leftover scraps. No solid "slices" of beef. Not one solid strip larger than 1/3 of an inch. The beef in here was one small step above ground beef. Won't ever get again. Disgusting.

reviews.title: Absolutely Not
reviews.text: I don't know where to start: The false advertising of 32 ounces of meat, or the grey meat being vacuum sealed into a puck instead of strips - perhaps the vaguely basic thickened soy sauce and sweetener? Even on sale at CostCo for $15 I would have been better off buying a steak and doing it myself, the only thing keeping me from returning it is I need something to eat for a quick lunch throughout the week.

reviews.title: Mongolian beef
reviews.text: I tried this today. I found the sauce to be very flavorful but was disappointed with the beef; it was extremely tough. A longer slower cook time would probably correct this. I would not buy this product again.

reviews.title: Delicious and Diabetic-Friendly
reviews.text: Just tried this one for the first time. My super-picky, diabetic husband kept RAVING about it! Quick to prepare, and absolutely delicious! We will definitely be looking for more flavors!

reviews.title: Different that photo
reviews.text: I think it throws people off because photo isn’t accurate but flavor is great! Maybe use a little salt.

reviews.title: So disappointed
reviews.text: I should start by saying that I am a big fan of the Kevin’s products. This is a different matter . The flavor is exceptional but the beef was like leather. It took forever to chew. I can’t help but hope this was a fluke bad batch. Everything else I have tried has been delicious.

reviews.title: Awful
reviews.text: I recently purchased your product, Sirloin Steak Tips With Gravy and found the taste of the gravy ok but I could not chew, much less swallow your "tender "sirloin tips. You say"fully cooked with gourmet cooking technique called sous-vide?" Change everything with your meat because the meat that was in my dinner, was nasty and darn sure not tasty!! If this is the way the rest of your dinners are, there are other companies that must be better??

reviews.title: Best one
reviews.text: I really love this one. My husband likes the teriyaki one, but I think that one has too much salt for me since I don’t really eat with a lot of salt but this one it’s perfect the honey mixed with the meat we make just simple rice bowls with it cause we try to eat pretty healthy and that’s kind of like a nice yummy treat and it doesn’t have a lot of salt so I’m really enjoy it.

reviews.title: Grade D- Beef
reviews.text: My college had food delivered to the dining hall labeled “Grade D Beef - Fit for Human Consumption”. All of my meals in college were better quality than the beef in this package.

reviews.title: What happened to the meat?!
reviews.text: Obviously some bean counter in the Corporate office decided to pad his bonus by sacrificing the quality of the meat. The first two times our family had this meal, we all enjoyed it very much. Two days ago, we had it again, and were shocked at the tough, gristly meat. I ate three bites and had to spit the last one out because I couldn't chew it. The whole family complained and were disappointed. I'm never buying the beef meals again, because I doubt they've confined the poor quality beef to just one entree. I will keep buying the chicken unless they ruin those, too.

reviews.title: Delicious!
reviews.text: Had this today for the first time - so good! Not too spicy at all - just right - lots of flavor and very tender. Will definitely purchase again.

reviews.title: This should be called orange beef
reviews.text: Very potent orange flavor; otherwise, lacking in flavor. I do really enjoy other Kevin's products so I was very disappointed in this. I also don't love orange flavor so I wouldn't have bought this if that was made more clear.

reviews.title: Beef may be sirloin but it’s stringy and has a lot of gristle
reviews.text: I really want to love the beef dishes but this is the third time I’ve bought one and it’s the worst yet. I dumped the whole thing because I couldn’t pull out any meat that wasn’t attached to long strings of gristle or tough connective tissue. Customer service implied that sirloin is high quality and that the quality controls in place should mean the beef is edible, BUT sirloin is only as good as its rating (select, choice, prime)—which was not specified, and clearly quality control doesn’t mean trimming the inedible bits off. Sorry, Kevin’s, but I won’t be back

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: I may be the exception, but this meal was the worst, tough pieces of meat full of grizzle.Very disappointed. No resemblance whatsoever to the photo on the box. In the future I’ll stick to the chicken entrees.

reviews.title: Wow
reviews.text: Thank you so much for this delicious beef dish! We eat this once or twice a month when I just can't spend time cooking. I do wish there was a bit more sauce as I like to add veggies and put it over rice. It is perfect for the 2 of us. Luckily I don't eat much. Never any left overs. We have tried other Kevin's meals they have all been great. I enjoy when the shop brings in another flavor.

reviews.title: Mongolian Beef will burn your mouth
reviews.text: My family and I could not eat the Mongolian beef cause it’s ridiculously spicy, which is not stated on the packaging. They need to list chili flakes on the list of ingredients. Our mouth was burning for a while after we had that initial first bite.

reviews.title: Bulgogi but
reviews.text: I have wanted to try this brand for a long time. Perhaps now that Mars owns them (2023 aquisition) they have a better and broader placement in grocery stores. I bought the Korean BBQ beef because there aren't many local places to get this. I think it was over sous vide - some of the beef was kind of mushy. Some of the beef was tough and/or stringy. Not all of it but enough I probably wouldn't try this meal again. I did purchase their Turkey, gravy, cranberry meal so we'll see how that fares. I think this is a great idea, an alternative to the delivery fresh meals at a better price with less waste.

reviews.title: LOVE THIS
reviews.text: I’ve been trying to eat healthier and this was the first Kevin’s meal I tried and it was delicious! Definitely going back to the store to get more!

reviews.title: Beef not up to par; flavor, amazing!
reviews.text: It’d be nice if the “gristle” in the beef was cut out before cooking. It makes breaking the sous vide meat into pieces difficult to impossible, and makes the chewing experience pretty frustrating. I think I pulled 8 pieces of gristle out this time before sautéing the meat in the pan. After that, the eating experience was amazing!

reviews.title: First time trying these delicious beef tips!
reviews.text: I baked potatoes and had fresh broccoli with these. They were delicious and my husband and I ate all of it! Yuka gave them a rating of 69 out of 100. So glad I tried them. Can’t wait to try the chicken one! I couldn’t take a picture because we ate it too fast!😊

reviews.title: Sirloin Steak Tips w/gravy
reviews.text: So delicious! Tender beef and perfectly seasoned gravy with a potato and salad, this will become my favorite meal!

reviews.title: Chimichurri Heaven!
reviews.text: This was EXCELLENT! This took less time than it took to make my rice! Dinner was ready in minutes, not hours. Steamed some broccoli on the side and we were good to go! Such a great idea for a quick and delicious meal for my family! THANK YOU!

reviews.title: Lone Star Hash
reviews.text: I make an excellent hash with Kevin’s Steak & Gravy as a base. Sweat 1/2 onion; 1 4oz. can Hatch mild Green Chilis; the Kevin’s steak cut into small pieces with juice; 2 tbs. chili powder (to taste); 1 tbs. paprika…cook the mix a bit, then add the gravy package; 20 oz. boxed chicken or beef stock; 1/2 cup long grain rice; 3 cups diced Idaho potatoes. Cook covered in 12” skillet ~20 minutes. Serve over unsweet corn bread.

reviews.title: SO Good!
reviews.text: This is the first one I tried and was blown away. The beef is perfect. The gravy is perfect. Served with green beans on the side and on top of a small amount of buttered noodles. Family loved it and bragged on the dinner. I haven't told them yet that I didn't actually make that.

reviews.title: HUGE hit at our house
reviews.text: This is our absolute favorite of all the Kevin's products we've tried. I sautee some red and yellow bell pepper and onion before searing the meat, then stir back in with the sauce. We serve it over rice or noodles, and have also added crushed peanuts on top, or stirred a spoonful of peanut butter into the sauce while simmering. Absolutely delicious. My picky picky husband asked to have it again a week after we first tried it. A sure winner, and so much healthier (and FASTER) than take out!

reviews.title: Too good to be low calorie
reviews.text: This dish is amazing! I stirfry snap peas or asparagus with garlic and ginger then add the beef. I have a hard time believing the calories count is only 180 prepared for 5oz!

reviews.title: Fast, easy and delicious
reviews.text: I normally cook everything from scratch but decided to try this after reading the ingredients at Sprouts. Sautéed some diced sweet onion in olive oil and butter and added jasmine rice for a base. I sautéed the beef per the instructions and finished it in the included sauce. Topped the rice with it along with some chopped fresh Cilantro and it was excellent!

reviews.title: Delish but spicy
reviews.text: This Mongolian beef has excellent flavor, but just a bit too spicy for my taste. We had it with rice, which calmed the burn. I recommend this entree but be aware of the heat.

reviews.title: SO easy—and delicious!
reviews.text: As someone who suffers from chronic fatigue, I'm constantly looking for easy ways to get a healthy meal on the table. When my local grocery ran a sale on Kevin's entrées, of course I wanted to try some. Wow, am I glad I did! I just sautéed some fresh (pre-riced) cauliflower in grapeseed oil, threw in a handful of (pre-shredded) carrots, a splash of rice vinegar and a little tamari, then put the BBQ beef on top with a tablespoon of chopped chives (I had to chop those, which was the hardest part lol). The whole thing couldn't have taken me more than 15 minutes, and it was absolutely delicious. Thank you so much for the assist! I'll definitely be buying this again.

reviews.title: My own DELICIOUS version of Beef with Broccoli
reviews.text: I purchased this at my local Smith’s grocery store on sale, having never tried it before. I cooked it up in toasted sesame oil. I also added a microwavable batch of sticky rice and some steamed frozen broccoli. I mixed the broccoli in with the beef and sauce toward the end of the heating process, and poured it over a scoop of sticky rice, added some more toasted sesame seeds for garnish with some red pepper flakes and YUM! I ate half of it yesterday and the other half reheated in microwave today… enjoyed with my chop sticks I felt like I was getting a restaurant quality meal for considerably less money!

reviews.title: Incredible
reviews.text: This is the best of the best! I’ve only had the garlic chicken and cilantro lime chicken, it’s good to know they sell it at target because that’s where I’ll be going. It’s so tender and full of flavor. It’s just incredible. Thank you for making this.

reviews.title: Full of flavor
reviews.text: I love every single Kevin’s meal that I try. I think the chimichurri beef became one of my faborites so far. Full of flavor and good quality meat.

reviews.title: OMG delicious
reviews.text: I purchased this and then saw the label that said it was soy free. My original thought was another health food. I actually went out and purchased a separate teriyaki sauce. When I opened the package and started making the beef I tried a little bit of the sauce and it is outrageously delicious. Thank you for making such a delicious meal. If I could find the teriyaki chicken, I will get that too.

reviews.title: Delicious!
reviews.text: This is the first time I’ve tried Kevin’s and it definitely won’t be the last! It was delicious and oh, so easy!

reviews.title: Beef tips
reviews.text: I add chopped onions, potatoes, red pepper and a little ketchup to sauce to sweeten it up, served with side of cauliflower or cabbage, delish

reviews.title: Yummy yummy for my tummy
reviews.text: One of my favorite dishes is beef Bulgogi. So when I saw this I knew I had to try it. I loved it! So much so that I ended up eating the entire thing myself instead of saving some for my lunch the next day. I will definitely be buying this again!

reviews.title: Yum!
reviews.text: Just had this with corn tortillas and a pickled cucumber salad I did on the fly and wow! Delicious! My family loved it 😋

reviews.title: Very tasty
reviews.text: Delicious and I served w green beans
First time buying the steak top meal and it's better than I expected! Super easy to cook and a quick meal! Added broccoli and rice and it was perfect. Will definitely buy more products from Kevin's

reviews.title: DELICIOUS
reviews.text: Stoked on the quality and the ingredients! Sometimes you just don't want to make a meal from scratch especially when you cook homemade all the time since it's hard to find quality ingredients in packaged made foods. Grateful this is now an option. Can't wait to try out more beef meals from Kevin's. Thank you!! Keep making clean meals for us people that care

reviews.title: Best Chimichurri Beef
reviews.text: I bought Kevin's Chimichurri Beef at Costco just to try it out. The entree exceeded my expectations! The meat was so very good and tender; and the sauce the best Chimichurri I've ever had. Entree was so good that I'm going back to stock up and freeze. I had frozen the one I had bought just before going on travel, defrosted it in the fridge, and today it was so very good. I will also try other entrees of Kevin's! Try it; you'll like it!

reviews.title: Oh My God
reviews.text: Tried your Mongolian-Style Beef on top of Panda Express' Chow Mein Noodles . . . I thought I was at a 5Star restaurant. Such a wonderful topping on any entree. You should be SSOOOOOO proud of this product. I can't wait to go back to the store and stock up. The beef was cooked to perfection and the sauce was amazing. Just the right amount of heat. Congratulations. I'm a convert! THANK YOU.

reviews.title: Great dinner with great flavor, yumm
reviews.text: Saw this at Costco and we love chimichurri so decided to try because we were short on time for cooking dinner. The chimichurri sauce was very good. Had a nice spice to it. I put it over shritaki noodles instead of noodles with the carbs. It was excellent, my husband loved it too. We will buy again. Every one of Kevin‘s meals we’ve tried we’ve loved 🥰

reviews.title: The #1 favorite
reviews.text: This one is the best one yet! So delicious my family can be picky and everyone loves it!

reviews.title: A great Healthy tasty alternative
reviews.text: This was first time purchasing this product. I’m on a low carb/ Keto diet and the sirloin tips with gravy was a good call. Combined it with broccoli florets and made broccoli beef that wasn’t gonna ruin my macros for the day. The meat was a tad bland but it was definitely tender and not dry. You can add additional spices of choice. The gravy came in s packet you add in once meat has been heated. It was delicious! The product was convenient easy and quick to prepare. Minimum fuss.Cost $9.89 at WalMart which for me is a tad expensive but healthier food products generally tend to be pricey. I would not hesitate to buy again in future coz I know it’s a quality product.I look forward to sampling a few more of Kevin’s products.

reviews.title: So thankful for these
reviews.text: I sent a bunch of these dinners down to my daughter who is going through full brain radiation for stage 4 melanoma. I live 2 states away from her and it is hard to know what to do to help her during her treatment. She is still trying to work part-time, but that mixed with radiation and chemo everyday leaves her with very little energy. These dinners were perfect. She can come home and do microwaved vegetables or a quick salad, add these dinners and have a healthy meal. This makes my daughter feel that life is a little normal plus she is having fun picking a different one to try each night. Now, I am considering sending some down to my 94 year old mom that still lives by herself. This way mom and her boyfriend can have a good healthy balanced meal with good protein. I have loved using Kevin's myself and I am now happy that they have allowed me to help my family.

When I first took the meat out of the package, I thought I detected some toughness. More on that in a bit. I sauteed some mushrooms to add to the meat, and I added them right after I added the sauce. I duly separated the food into two portions, planning to enjoy the leftovers (including sauteed zucchini) for lunch tomorrow. I devoured it! All the meat was tender and no weird softness like some meats get (if they are brined or "tenderized," for example, which of course Kevin's is not treated like that). It was so delicious; I ate both portions - oops. So much for leftovers. YUM!

reviews.title: Mongolian Beef
reviews.text: This entree is one of my favorites... ah, let's face it, I have several favorites... But this one will be in my next order... probably 2 or 3 will be, and some extra sauce. What makes this meal so appealing to me is the ease of opening the meat package, draining it, finishing it in a hot skillet, then adding the sauce. I usually make up a large pot of rice or bake a few potatoes, so all I have to do is add to the skillet or put the potato in the air fryer to brown and crisp it. All that is left to do - is add your favorite vegetable or a salad, and I end up with a tasty, filling meal. And the best part is that I have leftovers for another meal or two. Also, last but very important... the quality of the meat I have tried so far, beef or chicken, is tender and of good quality. If you are on the fence about ordering, get over it and do it... you will not be disappointed... and if you are well, you might just be hard to please.
